content-right-description0: !if you use the assembler in space switch docking direction to up!
content-right-description1:
Overview

A block needed for a launch pad to assemble any rocket and as a docking port on space stations.
content-right-description2:
Usage

Using a linker you can connect other components (terminals,rocket fluid/item input/output and fluid loaders) of the launch pad to the rocket (as an alternative to just linking them to the rocket) and to fly your rocket to the assembler (respects docking settings)

Assembling mode:
When placed next to a launchpad (at least 3x3) and a structure tower (at least 5 blocks high) the assembler will turn on (as indicated by the scanner resting inactive on the ground).
For the rocket to be assembeled you need a Guidance computer, a Fuel tank and a Rocket Engine
After building your rocket on the launch pad, you will need to click 'build' wich will scan the rocket and assemble it.
Note that you will require enrgy and that there is a 1 block padding zone in wich the rocket assembler won't asemble.

Info mode:
When a rocket is at the assembler it will display infos about the rocket including Mass, Thrust max and cur, Weight and Fuel, which will help getting the right configuration for your rocket.
There also is a 'test flight' button wich allows you to test flight the rocket to the assmebler.

Docking mode:
When placed in space the rocket assembler will enable the docking mode setting wich will as well configure how the rocket launches and lands.
The left button controlls from which direction the rocket launches, the left button will change the rockets Orienentation.
This setting is usefull for asteroid and gas mining missions.

Redstone capabbilities
Using a comparator you will get a signal if there is a rocket that alnded at the assembler.
